name: Enterprise AI Governance Hub
note: Estimates based on Azure retail pricing. Actual costs depend on usage, region, reserved instances, and enterprise agreements.

services.name: Azure API Management
services.purpose: AI governance gateway — centralized proxy for all AI model and agent API calls across the enterprise, policy enforcement (rate limits, content filtering, usage quotas), approval gate workflows for new model deployments, API versioning and deprecation management, developer portal for AI consumers, real-time usage analytics and chargeback metering

prod.note: Dedicated gateway, custom domains, policy engine

services.name: Azure Policy
services.purpose: Compliance enforcement — built-in and custom policy definitions for AI resource configurations, deny/audit/modify effects for non-compliant deployments, initiative assignments for regulatory frameworks (EU AI Act, NIST AI RMF, ISO 42001), compliance state tracking, remediation task automation

enterprise.note: Included with Azure subscription, custom initiatives

services.name: Azure Monitor
services.purpose: AI observability — centralized logging for all AI model invocations, token usage tracking per model/team/project, latency and error rate monitoring, cost attribution dashboards, compliance audit log aggregation, alert rules for policy violations and anomalous usage patterns

services.name: Azure Cosmos DB
services.purpose: Governance registry — AI model catalog (versions, capabilities, risk tiers, owners), agent registry with deployment status, approval workflow state machines, compliance assessment records, policy violation history, team entitlements and quota allocations, audit trail with immutable change history

enterprise.note: Multi-region writes, global distribution for hub-and-spoke governance

services.name: Azure Machine Learning
services.purpose: Model management — centralized model registry with risk classification, model card generation and maintenance, evaluation pipeline integration for pre-deployment gates, responsible AI dashboards (fairness, interpretability, error analysis), deployment tracking across environments

prod.note: Dedicated compute, managed endpoints, model registry

services.name: Key Vault
services.purpose: Governance secrets — AI service API keys under centralized management, model endpoint credentials, approval workflow signing keys, integration tokens for compliance tools, encryption keys for audit logs

services.name: Application Insights
services.purpose: Governance hub KPIs — approval workflow latency, policy compliance rates, model registry query performance, API gateway throughput, governance portal user engagement, compliance report generation times

optimization_tips: Use Azure Policy's free tier strategically — built-in AI governance policies (deny unapproved model deployments, audit content safety configuration, enforce managed identity) cost nothing and provide immediate compliance value before investing in custom policies

Implement tiered monitoring — log full request/response bodies only for high-risk AI models (GPT-4o, custom fine-tuned models) and log only metadata (tokens, latency, status) for low-risk models (embeddings, text-ada); reduces log ingestion volume by 70%

Use API Management consumption tier for dev/test governance gateways — production governance gateway needs dedicated capacity for reliability, but development environments rarely exceed 100K calls/month

Centralize Key Vault for AI keys — instead of each team managing their own AI service keys, use a shared governance Key Vault with RBAC; reduces Key Vault instances and operations costs while improving security posture

Schedule compliance reports generation during off-peak hours — aggregate compliance data from Cosmos DB change feed and generate reports via scheduled Azure Functions instead of real-time computation; batch processing is 80% cheaper than on-demand queries

Implement model registry caching — cache model catalog and compliance status in API Management's built-in cache; governance portal users repeatedly query the same model metadata, and a 5-minute cache TTL eliminates redundant Cosmos DB reads
Set Azure Cost Management budgets with alerts at 50%, 80%, 100%
